dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Wong Kar-wai / Christopher Doyle step-print romance lineage (In the Mood for Love, Chungking Express, Fallen Angels, 2046). Medium/era: 90s-2000s Hong Kong art-cinema, 35mm with step-printed motion. Named anchor: WKW step-print longing — smeared-time motion against a still, saturated world. Signature light: warm practical neon and tungsten in cramped interiors — a corridor bulb, a noodle-stall sign, a lamp through a beaded curtain; motivated, close, and intimate; the subject half-lit in warm pools against deep shadow. Color/grade: saturated jewel tones — emerald, crimson, amber, teal — rich and lush, never desaturated; a warm-amber-and-green interior romance palette. Lens/optics: 25-50mm, shallow-leaning but composed, foreground obstruction (a doorframe, a hanging lamp, a mirror) framing the subject; smeared step-printed motion blur on a passing figure while the subject stays still. Texture/render: 35mm grain, rich blacks, reflections and half-glimpses through glass and mirror; a voyeuristic framed-through-object composition. Composition+motion: the subject held in longing stillness while the world step-prints past in blurred smear-time; a fan turning, steam rising, a slow held gesture; intimacy and distance held at once.
anchor: WKW step-print longing: saturated jewel-tone neon-tungsten interiors, framed-through-object voyeur composition, still subject against step-printed smeared motion, warm intimate half-light, 35mm grain.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Wong Kar-wai's step-printed smear-time and saturated intimate-longing grade onto the chosen world; use for a romantic, yearning, framed-through-glass emotional beat.
avoid: NO Chow Mo-wan, NO Su Li-zhen, NO named Wong Kar-wai character, NO Maggie Cheung / Tony Leung likeness. NO specific film setting (1960s Hong Kong apartment, Chungking Mansions). NO desaturated grade, NO clean stabilized motion, NO teal-orange Hollywood grade.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Andrei Tarkovsky sculpted-time lineage (Stalker, Mirror, Nostalghia, Andrei Rublev, Solaris). Medium/era: 70s-80s Soviet art-cinema, long-take contemplative. Named anchor: Tarkovsky sculpted-time drift — the elemental long-take, water and earth as protagonists. Signature light: soft natural overcast or a single window shaft; the light is patient and even, revealing texture over drama; interiors lit by a lone window or fire, exteriors by diffuse grey sky. Color/grade: muted desaturated naturalism — wet greens, earth-browns, grey-blue; occasional shift to warm sepia for a memory register; never saturated, always weathered. Lens/optics: long slow takes on 35-50mm, deep patient focus, a drifting dolly at walking pace or slower; no cut where a slow move can carry the time. Texture/render: 35mm grain, water everywhere — dripping, pooling, flowing — earth, damp stone, peeling wall, all rendered with tactile patience; elemental physicality. Composition+motion: extremely slow — a long drift across a ruined interior, rain falling indoors, a candle carried across a room; time is the subject; the held elemental detail (water, fire, wind through grass) carries the spiritual weight.
anchor: Tarkovsky sculpted-time: elemental long-take drift at walking pace, patient soft natural light, muted weathered naturalism with sepia-memory shift, water/earth as protagonist, time as subject.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Tarkovsky's slow elemental long-take and patient weathered naturalism onto the chosen world; use for a contemplative, spiritual, time-dilated beat.
avoid: NO Stalker / Writer / Professor, NO named Tarkovsky character. NO specific film location (the Zone, the Solaris station). NO fast cutting, NO saturated grade, NO dramatic hard lighting, NO handheld shake.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Bong Joon-ho / Hong Kyung-pyo vertical-geometry lineage (Parasite, Snowpiercer, Mother, Memories of Murder). Medium/era: contemporary Korean prestige cinema, precise blocking. Named anchor: Bong vertical class-geometry — up/down staging as social meaning, precise architectural blocking. Signature light: motivated and controlled — a semi-basement window, a fluorescent stairwell, a bright upper-floor daylight versus a dim lower-floor; the light level itself encodes the vertical hierarchy. Color/grade: controlled naturalism with a slight cool lean, precise and clean, a warm accent where status warms; not desaturated, not stylized-saturated — exact. Lens/optics: 35-50mm, deep-focus staging front-to-back and top-to-bottom, stairs and level-changes composed into the frame; locked or precise slow moves. Texture/render: photoreal with clean prestige detail, architectural specificity (stairwells, thresholds, level-transitions), skin and surface honest. Composition+motion: vertical staging is the signature — a figure high, a figure low, a staircase cutting the frame, movement up or down encoding power; precise, geometric, deliberate; the blocking IS the meaning.
anchor: Bong vertical class-geometry: up/down architectural staging as social meaning, light-level encoding hierarchy, deep-focus top-to-bottom blocking, controlled clean naturalism, precise deliberate moves.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Bong's vertical-geometry blocking and light-as-hierarchy grammar onto the chosen world; use for a socially-charged, precisely-staged, up/down composition beat.
avoid: NO Ki-taek / Ki-woo / Park family, NO Curtis, NO named Bong Joon-ho character. NO specific film setting (the Park house, the Snowpiercer train, the semi-basement). NO handheld chaos, NO saturated stylization, NO teal-orange grade.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the George Miller / John Seale Fury Road center-frame chaos lineage. Medium/era: 2015 high-speed action cinema, saturated desert. Named anchor: Miller center-framed chaos — the eye locked center while everything explodes around it, day-for-desert saturation. Signature light: harsh overhead desert sun, hard and high, or a saturated blue-cyan night-for-desert; the light is punishing and clear, no softening; every subject sharply exposed against the vast environment. Color/grade: hyper-saturated teal-and-orange as a deliberate stylized signature (the one place it belongs) — searing orange sand against pushed cyan sky; bold, loud, saturated. Lens/optics: wide 21-35mm for the chase-scale, center-framed composition so the eye never hunts, fast whip-motion around a locked center; deep focus. Texture/render: dust, grit, chrome, and rust rendered at high fidelity, sun-flare and particulate everywhere, real practical dust and speed. Composition+motion: everything center-framed and eye-locked while the world blurs past at speed; a whip-pan chaos around a still center; kinetic, propulsive, but always readable because the subject holds center.
anchor: Miller Fury-Road chaos: eye-locked center-frame while the world blurs past at speed, harsh high desert sun, hyper-saturated teal-and-orange desert, wide deep-focus, propulsive whip-motion.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Fury Road's center-framed chaos-legibility and saturated-desert grade onto the chosen world; use for a high-speed, propulsive, kinetic action beat that must stay readable.
avoid: NO Max, NO Furiosa, NO Immortan Joe, NO named Mad Max character. NO War Rig, NO Interceptor car, NO Citadel, NO franchise vehicle or War Boy design. NO muted desaturated grade, NO slow contemplative pace, NO off-center hunt-for-subject framing.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Breaking Bad / Michael Slovis Southwest-procedural lineage (New Mexico high-desert grammar). Medium/era: 2008-2013 prestige television, wide-desert realism. Named anchor: Slovis rig-POV and time-lapse desert — the low-mounted object-POV lens, the amber high-desert vista. Signature light: harsh New Mexico sun, warm amber and hard; deep clean shadows; a single warm practical for interiors; the desert light is a character, punishing and clear. Color/grade: warm amber-and-tan desert dominant, sun-baked sienna and dust-gold, a clean deep shadow; a controlled warm naturalism, saturated by sun not by grade. Lens/optics: wide 18-28mm for the vista, and the signature low-mounted rig-POV (a lens mounted to an object, a barrel, a shovel, a car — object-eye view); occasional time-lapse cloud-shadow racing the landscape. Texture/render: high-fidelity dust, cracked earth, sun-bleached surface, heat-haze; photoreal grit. Composition+motion: the tiny human against the vast amber desert, a low object-POV looking up, a time-lapse of cloud-shadow sweeping the plain; stillness and scale, or a precise procedural insert.
anchor: Slovis desert-procedural: low-mounted object-POV rig lens, amber high-desert vista, harsh warm New Mexico sun, cloud-shadow time-lapse, tiny human against vast scale, sun-saturated warm naturalism.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Breaking Bad's rig-POV object-eye framing and amber high-desert scale onto the chosen world; use for a sun-baked, procedural, wide-Southwest beat with an inventive lens angle.
avoid: NO Walter White, NO Jesse Pinkman, NO Gus Fring, NO Saul, NO named Breaking Bad character. NO RV lab, NO Los Pollos Hermanos, NO franchise-specific prop or setting. NO cool desaturated grade, NO teal-orange Hollywood push, NO soft flat light.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Severance / Jessica Lee Gagné corporate-uncanny lineage (Lumon severed-floor grammar). Medium/era: 2022+ prestige television, sterile retro-futurist. Named anchor: severed-floor maze — the endless white corridor, retro-uncanny symmetry, dread through blandness. Signature light: flat cold institutional fluorescent, even and shadowless, filling every corner so nothing hides; the uniform brightness IS the dread; no warm bounce, no drama, clinical evenness. Color/grade: desaturated cold institutional palette — bland teal-green carpet, cold white walls, muted retro accent (a dull blue, a faded green); the blandness deliberate and oppressive. Lens/optics: wide 24-35mm one-point-perspective corridor, dead-center symmetry, slow motorized dolly down the endless hallway; deep focus; the architecture is the maze. Texture/render: clean prestige photoreal, retro-corporate detail (CRT-era terminals, laminate desks, drop-ceiling grids), sterile and precise. Composition+motion: one-point-perspective corridor with the vanishing line dead-center, a small figure in the vast bland maze, a slow forward dolly of approach-dread; symmetry and uniform light generating unease from the ordinary.
anchor: Severance corporate-dread: endless one-point white corridor, flat cold shadowless fluorescent, desaturated institutional blandness, dead-center symmetry, slow forward dolly, retro-uncanny detail.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Severance's sterile corporate-maze symmetry and shadowless-fluorescent dread onto the chosen world; use for an uncanny, institutional, bland-oppressive beat.
avoid: NO Mark S., NO Helly, NO Milchick, NO named Severance character. NO Lumon logo, NO the exact severed-floor set or MDR office, NO franchise-specific iconography. NO warm cozy light, NO saturated palette, NO handheld, NO dramatic shadow.

dna: Photoreal live-action still in the Chernobyl / Jakob Ihre Soviet-procedural lineage (HBO 2019 grammar). Medium/era: prestige television historical reconstruction, documentary-adjacent realism. Named anchor: Ihre muted-procedural dread — Sovcolor-tinted desaturation, institutional weight, documentation over expression. Signature light: available-light naturalism — overcast grey exteriors, cold institutional fluorescent or a lone incandescent interior; soft, real, unglamorous; the light documents rather than dramatizes. Color/grade: near-total desaturation with a faint sickly green-grey Sovcolor undertone in shadow, a dim warm amber as the only warmth signal; muted, cold, photochemical; saturation would feel dishonest. Lens/optics: 35-50mm, restrained composition, locked or slow deliberate moves, deep documentary focus; no stylized angle. Texture/render: high-fidelity period grit — concrete, worn fatigues, aged plaster, radiation-grain; photoreal weathered surface. Composition+motion: procedural stillness, a figure small against institutional architecture, held documentary observation; the dread is in the muted matter-of-factness, one dim warm accent carrying disproportionate weight.
anchor: Ihre Soviet-procedural dread: near-total desaturation with sickly green-grey Sovcolor shadow, available-light naturalism, one dim amber warmth, documentary framing, institutional matter-of-fact weight.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Chernobyl's muted Sovcolor procedural-dread and documentary-realism restraint onto the chosen world; use for a grave, institutional, historically-weighted beat.
avoid: NO Legasov, NO Khomyuk, NO Dyatlov, NO named Chernobyl character. NO the specific reactor set or control room, NO franchise-specific reconstruction. NO saturated color, NO glamour lighting, NO stylized angle, NO teal-orange grade.

dna: Fine-art portrait lighting in the Rembrandt van Rijn chiaroscuro lineage (Dutch Golden Age painting grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: 17th-century oil-painting light logic applied as a photographic/render lighting doctrine. Named anchor: Rembrandt triangle — the single soft window key, the luminous face emerging from deep warm dark. Signature light: one soft directional key from high and to the side (a north window), producing the signature small illuminated triangle on the shadow-side cheek; the shadow side falls into rich warm darkness; no fill except a faint reflected warmth; the face glows, the ground recedes. Color/grade: warm old-master palette — umber and burnt-sienna shadow, warm flesh in the light, a deep near-black warm background; a single saturated accent (a red sleeve, a gold chain) as the painting's jewel. Lens/optics: 85-105mm portrait compression, shallow-leaning focus on the eyes, the background dissolving into warm dark; painterly falloff. Texture/render: oil-painting surface logic — luminous skin with visible warm/cool modeling, impasto highlight on the catchlight, deep glazed shadow; the rendering feels painted, not photographed. Composition+motion: a still, dignified portrait — the subject three-quarter turned, emerging from the dark, the catchlight in the eye; stillness and inward presence; almost no motion, the light doing the work.
anchor: Rembrandt chiaroscuro: single high-side soft window key, shadow-cheek triangle, luminous face from deep warm dark, umber old-master palette with one jewel accent, 85mm painterly falloff, dignified stillness.
use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Rembrandt's single-key chiaroscuro and warm old-master portrait falloff onto the chosen world; use for a dignified, luminous, painterly single-subject portrait beat.
avoid: NO reproduction of a specific Rembrandt painting or its named sitter (The Night Watch figures, self-portraits). NO Dutch-Golden-Age costume pastiche as a requirement. NO flat even lighting, NO cool grade, NO saturated modern palette, NO hard clinical light.
